name: Beef Stock- Master Chefs
recipeIngredient: 2 medium Onions (trimmed, quartered - do not peel)
1 leek (trimmed lengthwise, halved - coarsely chopped white and green parts)
2 medium Tomatoes (fresh or canned, cored, coarsely chopped)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Place oil in a roasting pan and heat briefly in the oven.
3. Add bones to the oil in the pan, tossing to coat and roast for 35 minutes.
4. Add onions, carrots, celery, leek, garlic, and parsley, tossing them all to coat with fat. Roast for an additional 30 minutes.
5. Remove the pan from the oven and transfer bones and vegetables to a clean stockpot. Drain as much of the fat as possible.
6. Place the roasting pan over medium-high heat (use two burners if necessary), adding 2 cups cold water and boiling briefly. Scrape up all browned bits into the water.
7. Transfer liquid to the stock pot and add enough cold water to cover. Bring slowly to a boil, skimming froth that forms.
8. Lower heat and add tomatoes, thyme, bay leaves, cloves, and salt. Simmer uncovered for 6-8 hours, adding water as necessary just to cover ingredients. Skim whenever needed.
9. Add peppercorns for the last 15 minutes of simmering.
10. Strain "soup" through a colander lined with a double layer of dampened cheesecloth into a large bowl. Gently press solids to extract liquid, discarding solids.
11. Pour stock into containers for storage and label/date them. The stock keeps up to 3 days in refrigerator and up to 6 months in freezer.
